About CTeBS

CTeBS (Classic Technologies & Business Solutions) is a technology company building an integrated ecosystem of software products for the fashion manufacturing industry. As a subsidiary of Classic Fashion, one of the leading garment manufacturers, CTeBS builds software products that are first developed, implemented, and matured within Classic Fashion's own manufacturing operations, and subsequently offered to external customers across the industry. This unique model means our products are battle-tested in real production environments before they reach the market, giving us deep domain credibility and a genuine understanding of manufacturing floor challenges.

Role Overview

We are looking for a Senior Product Manager to lead and grow the Product Management function at CTeBS. This is a player-coach role — you will directly own one or more software products while also providing leadership, mentorship, and strategic direction to the existing PM team of 3. The ideal candidate combines strong product management expertise with hands-on knowledge of manufacturing operations — ideally in apparel, garments, textiles, or closely adjacent industries (footwear, home textiles).

Key Responsibilities

Product Ownership & Strategy

  • Own the end-to-end product lifecycle for one or more CTeBS software products — from discovery and ideation through development, launch, and iteration.
  • Define and maintain product roadmaps aligned with both Classic Fashion's internal needs and external market opportunities.
  • Conduct market research, competitive analysis, and customer discovery to identify product-market fit for external commercialisation.
  • Drive the transition of internally-matured products into market-ready offerings for external garment manufacturing customers.
  • Establish and track product KPIs, OKRs, and success metrics to measure product health and business impact.

Team Leadership & Mentorship

  • Lead, mentor, and develop a team of 3 product professionals with varying experience levels.
  • Establish product management best practices, frameworks, and processes across the team.
  • Conduct regular 1:1s, product reviews, and sprint retrospectives to ensure quality and accountability.
